content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Jun 12, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development focused on finalizing the Eliza Cloud Apps infrastructure. Key achievements include the deployment of the Caddy ingress front door and the stabilization of the control-plane via automated Headscale installation. Progress is underway to resolve state-management blockers to enable the apps-data-plane go-live.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Cloud Infrastructure & Ingress**
*   Implemented the Caddy front door in the `app-node` cloud-init, replacing the final TODO and enabling ingress for the staging app node ([#8394](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8394)).
*   Automated the installation of Headscale in the `control-plane` cloud-init, streamlining the production migration process ([#8393](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8393)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#8400](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8400): Added a one-shot workflow to remove orphaned apps-data-plane state entries.
*   [#8399](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8399): Dependency update for `esbuild` (0.28.0 to 0.28.1).
*   [#8398](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8398): Created a dispatchable workflow to arm the apps daemon without requiring manual operator SSH.
*   [#8397](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8397): Added an idempotent script for arming the apps-daemon.
*   [#8396](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8396): Dependency update for `pypdf` (6.10.2 to 6.12.0).
*   [#8395](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8395): Added EvoLink support to `plugin-openai`.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   None.

## NEW ISSUES
*   None.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   [#8321](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8321) **Eliza Cloud Apps: scaling + ingress gaps before go-live**
    *   Development has shifted to driving the apps go-live process.
    *   A state-provider mismatch (orphaned `random_password` resource) was identified as the primary blocker for the `apps-data-plane` apply.
    *   New IaC-based workflows ([#8400](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8400), [#8398](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8398)) have been proposed to resolve state issues and arm the daemon without manual intervention.
    *   The team plans to proceed with staging verification, including node replacement and sample app deployment, before moving to production.
